title: Http Server Static Ui
description: When `specd ui serve` runs, the browser loads Studio from the same origin as the API so the connect gate can be skipped. This spec defines how the Node server serves the built `@specd/ui` dist: static assets with correct content types and SPA fallback for client-side deep links.
context: When `specd ui serve` runs, the browser loads Studio from the same origin as the API so the connect gate can be skipped. This spec defines how the Node server serves the built `@specd/ui` dist: static assets with correct content types and SPA fallback for client-side deep links.

rules.requirement: static assets are served from configured dist path
rules: Given `uiDistPath`, the server MUST serve files from that directory at `/` (or configured base) with correct content types.
Unknown non-API GET paths that look like client routes MUST fall back to `index.html` so React Router can handle deep links.

constraints: @specd/api delivery and composition code MUST import host bootstrap and kernel types from @specd/sdk, not @specd/core or @specd/code-graph directly.
HTTP handlers MUST NOT import @specd/core from @specd/ui or @specd/client.
v1 server auth: api.auth.type from specd.yaml (never studio.*); registry registers only disabled; no server-side Bearer enforcement on loopback or specd ui serve.
Artifact save/load MUST use core:save-change-artifact and core:get-change-artifact — not raw ChangeRepository.saveArtifact from HTTP handlers.
There is no GET /changes/{name}/validation resource; use GET .../status and POST .../validate.
Canonical workspace spec artifacts are read-only in Studio v1.

scenarios.requirement: static assets are served from configured dist path
scenarios.name: JS bundle served from ui-dist
given: ui-dist points at built Studio assets

scenarios.requirement: static assets are served from configured dist path
scenarios.name: Missing asset returns 404
then: AND not HTML error page from API router

scenarios.requirement: static assets are served from configured dist path
scenarios.name: Custom ui-dist flag overrides default
then: files are read from custom directory

scenarios.name: API paths are not captured by SPA fallback
